figures.caption: Fig. 1. Noise parameter measurement stand consists of microscope 1, cryostat 2, control computer 3, vector network analyzer (VNA) 4, temperature readout 5, source-measure units (SMU) 6 for DC-biasing.

Fig. 2. Electrical block-diagram of the measurement instruments and hard- ware. Two major components are the VNA (Keysight 5242B) and the cryo wafer prober (Form Factor), in between there are minor room temperature components amplifier 22 (Mini-Circuits) and directional coupler 32. The VNA equivalent circuit includes: RF source 1; terminated switches 2,10,11,18; SPDT switch 20; heterodyne receivers R1...R4, A,B,C,D; noise receiver 21; and directional couplers 3,6,9,14,17,19. The RF hardware inside the cryo- stat includes: 4-12 GHz triple junction ferrite isolator 23, 3.4-12.3 GHz circulator 28, 0.3-14 GHz low noise amplifier (Low noise factory) 30, 30 dB directional coupler 24, 0.2-18 GHz bias-T 27 (Quantum Microwave), impedance tuner 25 with built in bias-T 26 (Maury Microwave), attenuator 31, wafer probes P1, P2, P3 (GGB Industries) and sample holder 29.

Fig. 3. Cryogenic instrumentation mounted on the 4K plate of the refrigerator: front view and top view (as seen by the microscope), with the same numbering as in block diagram in fig.2. Probes P1, P2, P3 are moved by Attocube piezo- based drives for cryogenic environments.
Fig. 6. Measured probe P2 source impedance as seen by the DUT (a) and with 1.923 ns delay subtracted (b): tuner output impedances Z1 red (center), Z2 green (right), Z3 blue (top), Z4 black (left).
Fig. 4. Signal graph of the system representing DUT, error boxes and five calibration planes required for calibration of 24 error terms.
Fig. 5. Characterization of the pre-amplifier. (a) preamp is connected to the tuner, (b) preamp is connected to the P1 for S-parameter measurement
Fig. 7. Measured S-parameters of the preamplifier including probe P3, bias- T 27 and circulator 28. Traces in (a): top: T50, bottom: Tmin; traces in (b) top to bottom: Sa21, Sa11, Sa22, Sa12.
Fig. 8. Measured Noise parameters of the preamplifier chain. Traces in (a): top: 4N, bottom: Fmin −1. Traces in (b): top: Ropt, bottom: jXopt.
Fig. 9. FET noise temperatures (a): top: T50, bottom: Tmin and S-parameters (b) top to bottom: Sa21, Sa11, Sa22, Sa12.

Fig. 10. Noise parameters of the FET: (a) Noise Factor (bottom) and Lange invariant (top), showing proper inequality 4N > Fmin −1, see [15]; (b) optimal resistance (bottom) and reactance of the source (top).
Fig. 11. The inequality 2 > 4NT0/Tmin > 1 provides a useful check to validate the measured noise parameters.
Fig. 12. Measurement of the FET (a) and LNA based on this FET (b)
Fig. 13. LNA noise temperatures (a): top: T50, bottom: Tmin and S- parameters (b) top to bottom: Sa21, Sa11, Sa22, Sa12.
Fig. 14. Noise parameters of the LNA: (a) Noise Factor (bottom) and Lange invariant (top); (b) optimal resistance (top) and reactance of the source (bottom).
Fig. 15. 3-stage common-source LNA schematic, the ”in”, ”out”, ”Vg1”, ”VDD”, and ”Vg23” octagons correspond to the pads on the die for probe landing.

Fig. 16. LNA noise temperature measured by the setup in this paper (bottom trace) and by the scalar on-wafer method from [7] (top trace), the latter trace noise is in agreement with ±6 K uncertainty demonstrated in [7] for input match of -10...-15 dB.

Fig. 17. Raw noise power spectral density produced by the FET and preamplifier in 4 impedance states of the tuner, as measured by the noise receiver. If counting traces from left to right, trace whose peak is the 1st corresponds to Z2, 2nd: Z3, 3rd: Z4, remaining trace in the center: Z1.
